title: ourisia macrocarpa subsp. calycina
intro: Ourisia macrocarpa subsp. calycina is a subspecies of the Ourisia macrocarpa plant, commonly known as the broad-leaved forget-me-not or New Zealand forget-me-not. This plant is a member of the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is native to New Zealand and Australia. The medicinal properties of Ourisia macrocarpa subsp. calycina include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions, and provide general health benefits due to its antioxidant and immune-boosting properties.
